_comment: Curated overlay appended to llms.txt by generate_llms_txt.py. Hand-maintained links the mechanical source-2.0 walk cannot produce: prominent in-spec references (including backward compatibility), example projects, per-language code generators, and tooling. Links prefer raw README/markdown (LLM-readable text) over repo landing pages, and use each repo's real default branch (smithy-python is 'develop', others 'main'). Notes are deliberately coarse and durable (lifecycle role, language, client/server scope, maturity) so they do not drift with each release; point at the repo for current feature detail. All URLs verified to resolve (HTTP 200).

sections.heading: Key references
sections.note: The pages most often needed when authoring or evolving a model.

links.title: Trait index (every trait, one page)
links.note: rendered index; the .rst source is a build-time directive

links.title: Evolving Models (backward compatibility guide)
links.note: how to change a model without breaking customers

sections.heading: Examples
sections.note: Runnable projects. Start from the index, then the per-language example.

links.title: smithy-examples (index of all examples)
links.note: quickstart, conversion, custom traits, projections, shared models, linting

links.title: awesome-smithy (community resources)
links.note: curated list of build tools, generators, and resources

sections.heading: Code generators by language
sections.note: Pick the generator for your target language; the README is the entry point. Consult each repo for the current list of supported protocols and features.

links.title: smithy-rs
links.note: codegen (Rust): client, server, and the AWS SDK for Rust; stable

links.title: smithy-typescript
links.note: codegen (TypeScript): client and server (SSDK); in development

links.title: smithy-python
links.note: codegen and core modules (Python); in development - see ROADMAP below

links.title: smithy-python roadmap
links.note: current status and planned Python features

links.title: smithy-cli
links.note: build, validate, diff, and select over a model
